decisions.decision: Replay matching EventTrade fills in ascending `(filledAt, id)` order so cost-basis reconstruction is deterministic even when `ORDERS` insertion order is arbitrary or multiple fills share the same timestamp.
alternatives_rejected: Rely on raw `ORDERS` dict iteration order.
Sort only by `filledAt` and leave ties unstable.
Use `createdAt` as the secondary tiebreaker even though it is often identical to `filledAt`.

decisions.decision: Keep `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` as the sole liveness gate for `/v1/accounts/{id}/positions`, and when a live row has no replayable filled EventTrade history, still serialize it with `unrealizedPnl: 0.0` instead of skipping the row or failing the request.
alternatives_rejected: Drop live rows whose order history is missing or incomplete.
Fail `/positions` when replay cannot recover a basis for one row.
Rebuild the open-position set from `ORDERS` alone.

decisions.decision: Scope replay to `ORDERS` rows with `type == "EventTrade"`, `status == "filled"`, and matching `accountId`, `targetMarketId`/`targetOutcomeId`, falling back to `marketId` plus the payload literal outcome when the target fields are absent.
alternatives_rejected: Scan every order type stored in `ORDERS`.
Treat order history as authoritative for whether a position is live.

decisions.decision: Implement signed average-cost replay as a small state machine: same-side fills resize the position and recompute weighted average cost, partial offsets preserve the prior basis on the surviving same-sign remainder, full closes zero the basis, and zero-crossing offsets reset the opposite-sign survivor's basis to the crossing fill price.
alternatives_rejected: Carry the pre-flip basis through a sign change.
Switch to FIFO or LIFO lots for this endpoint.

decisions.decision: Compute wire-level P&L from the account perspective as `unrealizedPnl = (mark - averageCost) * netSize`, using `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(marketId, outcomeId)` for the current mark and preserving positive P&L for profitable shorts via the signed `netSize` convention.
alternatives_rejected: Use separate long and short formulas.
Mark against `lastTradePrice` instead of the current inference probability.
Expose basis or side fields on the wire instead of one signed P&L scalar.

decisions.decision: Test replay math by patching `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(...)` in the representative `/positions` regressions so assertions isolate order replay and P&L math from unrelated inference behavior.
alternatives_rejected: Assert against live fixture-market inference values in all replay regressions.
Skip explicit P&L regressions and rely only on end-to-end route smoke tests.

steps.task: Add backend helper utilities in `backend/server.py` to resolve one exposure row's `(marketId, outcomeId)` slice, filter matching filled EventTrade orders from `ORDERS`, and return them in stable `(filledAt, id)` replay order.

Implement a read-time replay accumulator in `backend/server.py` that walks the ordered fills for one live slice and derives the surviving signed `netSize`, signed average cost, and a 'history found' signal while handling same-side accumulation, partial offsets, full closes, and sign flips.

Thread the replay helper into the positions serializer/read model so each live row keeps the existing liveness and lexical ordering behavior, falls back to `unrealizedPnl: 0.0` when no replayable history exists, and otherwise marks the surviving basis to `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(...)` with the `(mark - averageCost) * netSize` formula.

Extend `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` with focused regressions for deterministic fill ordering, missing-history fallback on directly seeded exposure rows, multi-fill weighted-average accumulation, partial close survivors, and sign-flip survivors, patching the mark query seam where P&L expectations need stable prices.

Run the targeted backend tests covering `/v1/accounts/{id}/positions` and the new replay helpers, confirm no contract regressions in the serialized positions shape, and then commit the implementation.
